data.title: ISEC Healthcare Obtains Operating License from Malaysian Ministry of Health
data.description:
ISEC Healthcare obtained an operation license from the Ministry of Health Malaysia to operate an eye specialist center, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

The center will provide ambulatory care service in Selangor, Malaysia.

data.title: Palm Oil Trades Notably Higher
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures jumped around 1% to above MYR 4,500 per tonne after touching a low of MYR 4,454 in the previous session, supported by data from the Solvent Extractors' Association of India (SEA) showing that India's palm oil imports surged 35.7% from the prior month to 373,549 metric tons in January.

Meanwhile, Malaysian Palm Oil Board's latest data showed that inventories fell for the fifth straight month to 1.51 million tons in February, the lowest since April 2023, while production dropped 4.16% to a three-year low of 1.19 million tons as floods disrupted output.

However, gains were capped by signs of declining exports, as cargo surveyors reported a 25.8%-38.3% plunge in Malaysian palm oil shipments in the first ten days of March.

In February, industry data noted shipments fell 16.27% to a four-year low of 1 million tons.

Additionally, uncertainties lingered over the impact of President Donald Trump's tariff policies on global markets, including palm oil.

data.title: Couche-Tard Doubles Down on Friendly Takeover of 7-Eleven Owner
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

TOKYO-Alimentation Couche-Tard will continue its friendly pursuit of Seven & i, executives said, sounding confident that they can overcome antitrust issues that could block the prospective acquisition in the U.S.

"We are continuing to pursue a friendly, mutually agreeable transaction" with the Japanese owner of the 7-Eleven convenience-store chain, Alain Bouchard, Couche-Tard's founder and executive chairman of the board, said at a news conference in Tokyo on Thursday.

"There is a clear path to regulatory approval," he said, while expressing frustration with the limited progress on broader deal discussions with Seven & i.

Couche-Tard Chief Executive Alex Miller said he has no plan for store closures or job cuts in Japan. "I think there's a lot of ways to increase profitability besides closing stores or firing people," he said, adding that listening to franchisees is one way to do so.

In a statement released earlier this week, the Canadian owner of Circle K reiterated its commitment to the deal, adding that there is considerable uncertainty around whether Seven & i's latest business plan can deliver value to shareholders. It also expressed disappointment that the engagement with Seven & i has been mostly focused on U.S. regulatory approval.

In September, Seven & i rejected an initial $39 billion buyout bid from Couche-Tard, saying the proposal underestimated the company's value and citing a possible antitrust challenge. Couche-Tard later raised its offer to about $47 billion.

The Canadian company said this week it resubmitted a revised, yen-denominated, nonbinding proposal on Jan. 24.

In a statement on a website, the company said it remains confident that its proposal is "the most attractive for Seven & i, its customers, franchisees, and the shareholders of both companies."

The 7-Eleven parent last week named its first American chief executive, former Walmart executive Stephen Hayes Dacus, and announced a plan to split its North American convenience stores into a separate listed company by the second half of next year.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: MTM Critical Metals Teams Up With Vedanta for Alumina Recycling Technology; Shares Up 10%
data.description:
MTM Critical Metals signed a memorandum of understanding with Vedanta to apply its proprietary flash joule heating technology for Vedanta's Red Mud alumina waste recycling project, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The company said Red Mud tailings have above-ground deposits of aluminum, titanium, gallium, scandium, and rare earth elements.

Financial terms were not disclosed.

The company's shares rose 10% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: Umm Al-Qura Cement Logs Higher FY24 Net Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Umm Al-Qura Cement (SASE:3005) said Wednesday net profit and revenue for the full-year 2024 jumped year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 47.7 million Saudi riyals, compared with 4 million riyals a year ago. EPS moved to 0.87 riyal from 0.07 riyal.

The Saudi Arabia-listed cement manufacturer's revenue was 265.6 million riyals, compared with 168.9 million riyals earlier.

data.title: Swire Pacific Declares Second Interim Dividend of HK$2.10 Per A Share, HK$0.42 Per B Share >0019.HK
China Zheshang Bank Information Chief Resigns
data.description:
China Zheshang Bank said Wang Chaoming resigned as chief information officer.

Wang will continue to serve as assistant to the president, according to a Wednesday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

data.title: Al Ramz Unit to Provide Liquidity Provision Services to Fertiglobe
data.description:
Al Ramz Corp. Investment and Development's (DFM:ALRAMZ) unit Al Ramz Capital said Wednesday it was appointed as a liquidity provider by Abu Dhabi-based fertilizer company Fertiglobe (ADX:FERTIGLB).

As agreed, the financial services provider will independently trade Fertiglobe's shares from Wednesday after securing regulatory approvals.

data.title: Market Chatter: Haidilao to Compensate Over 4,000 Diners for Broth Pee Incident; Shares Fall 3%
data.description:
Haidilao will compensate more than 4,000 customers who visited its downtown Shanghai restaurant after an incident in which two individuals urinated into a hotpot broth, Reuters reported Wednesday.

A video of two men peeing on the broth of their hotpot in a private dining room went viral in February, the report said.

Haidilao said it only learned of the incident four days after it occurred on Feb. 24 and initially had no information about the time or location, Reuters reported.

The hotpot giant said the incident revealed shortcomings in staff training, the news agency reported.

Haidilao did not disclose how much it would compensate each affected customer, according to Reuters.

Shares fell 3% during Thursday's late morning trading.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Yamada Green Resources Increases Registered Capital of Subsidiary
data.description:
Yamada Green Resources received approval to increase the registered capital of a wholly owned subsidiary to 80 million yen, effective Feb. 21, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

The move is not expected to impact the company's earnings per share for the current financial year.

data.title: Market Chatter: MBK Partners Under Tax Probe Amid Homeplus Fallout
data.description:
South Korea's National Tax Service launched a tax investigation into private equity firm MBK Partners, the majority shareholder of Homeplus, following the retailer's abrupt corporate rehabilitation filing, Pulse News reported Wednesday.

The probe, which involves a team of seven to eight inspectors, is being led by the Seoul Regional Tax Office's Investigation Division 4.

It raises concerns about potential tax evasion, given the division's focus on special audits, the report said.

The NTS may have obtained evidence warranting further scrutiny of MBK's financial activities, the report said, citing industry sources.

In a 2020 audit, MBK was fined 42 billion won for offshore tax evasion, according to the report.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Singapore iron ore rebounds as near-term China demand stays resilient
data.description:
Singapore iron ore futures rebounded on Thursday, driven by a wave of short-covering as near-term demand in top consumer China stayed resilient, although concerns over the global trade conflict limited the upside trend.

The benchmark April iron ore (SZZFJ5) on the Singapore Exchange was up 0.83% at $101.5 a metric ton, as of 0325 GMT.

"Prices are basically moving between $95 and $110 a ton, and every time when they fall below $100, there will be a rebound soon due to short-covering activities," a Beijing-based iron ore trader said, requesting anonymity as he is not authorised to speak to media.

"In the short term, hot metal output is still recovering, so it's unlikely to see a drastic fall in prices although downward pressure will be more intense in the second half of the year."

Hot metal output, typically used to gauge iron ore demand, will pick up further in March due to handsome steel margins, analysts at Hongyuan Futures said in a note.

The most-traded May iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) , however, closed morning trade 0.13% lower at 775.5 yuan ($107.18) a ton.

Prices of the key steelmaking ingredient retreated on Wednesday as market sentiment was dampened by renewed talks of China's plan to cut crude steel production in an effort to address the oversupply issues plaguing the industry.

State planner China's National Development and Reform Commission did not respond to Reuters' request for comment.

Other steelmaking ingredients on the DCE advanced, with coking coal NYMEX:ACT1! and coke (DCJcv1) up 1.59% and 1.14%, respectively.

Steel benchmarks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange gained ground. Rebar rose 0.71%, hot-rolled coil added 0.89%, wire rod (SWRcv1) climbed 0.7% and stainless steel edged 0.33% higher.

($1 = 7.2357 Chinese yuan)

data.title: New Zealand Shares Slip 0.3% for Third Straight Loss — Market Talk
data.description: New Zealand's NZX-50 closed 0.3% lower at 12209.05, falling into the red late in the session to stay firmly on course for a fourth straight weekly decline. The benchmark index was 0.4% higher after about two hours of Thursday's session, but pared its gains before dropping away midway through the afternoon. Five of the seven largest stocks by market capitalization finished lower, with medical device maker Fisher & Paykel and infrastructure investor Infratil shedding 1.1% and 1.7%, respectively. Aged-care providers Summerset, Ryman and Oceania gave up between 2.1% and 3.1%. The NZX-50's third straight daily decline left it 1.5% lower so far this week. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: India’s FIU Approves Coinbase, Millions of Customers Benefit from Increased Competition
data.description:
Coinbase has registered with India’s Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU), allowing it to legally trade in the country. The main regulatory body supports Coinbase and adheres to rules to limit money laundering on the platform when trading in India.

The FIU approval is a significant step for Coinbase and the crypto community because Indian traders can now access one of the most reliable exchanges on the market, a possible gateway for new retail traders.

Binance and other exchanges, like CoinDCX, already operate in the country with regulatory approval. The FIU is India’s anti-money laundering watchdog and oversees all crypto approvals in the country.

In 2022, Coinbase ceased operations in India due to unfavourable regulatory conditions, a trend at the time, with many exchanges facing extreme pressure from regulatory bodies. At the time, Brian Armstrong, Coinbase CEO, said that the exchange had to exit the country because of pressure exerted by the Indian central bank.

“India represents one of the most exciting market opportunities in the world today”, wrote John O’Loghlen, Coinbase Asia Pacific director, “and we’re proud to deepen our investment here in full compliance with local regulations”.

Competition will benefit in India, extending to the digital asset industry. Coinbase, Binance, KuCoin, and CoinDCX, all approved by the FIU, can now focus on customer satisfaction rather than operating in an anti-competitive environment.

“India represents”, wrote Coinbase official blog, “one of the most exciting market opportunities in the world today. The country has rapidly grown into an on-chain development hub, with its share of global developers increasing from 3% in 2018 to 12% in 2023. Among emerging markets, India now boasts the highest concentration of talent in the on-chain space”.

Younger investors are particularly interested in crypto, which is part of the impetus for approving the exchange. Many Indian youths are looking to crypto as an alternate financial system that offers the opportunity for investment and monetary freedom. The debate continues regarding the regulatory framework of crypto in India, yet the use and development of crypto technology continue to become a growth area for the country’s economy.

The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) exerted a lot of pressure on the crypto market, especially international companies, to encourage locally run businesses to thrive and prevent international rackets from conducting money laundering operations. A debate started about the strict measures the Indian Reserve Bank took, as international exchanges like Binance and KuCoin were banned, to ascertain a fine balance between monetary freedom and regulatory safety.

In 2024, Chainalysis concluded, after extensive analysis, that India had the highest crypto adoption rate globally, with millions of people participating in the market. Therefore, overcoming this regulatory hurdle would be essential to extending Bitcoin’s influence worldwide. 

According to a 2024 study by Chainalysis, India has the highest cryptocurrency adoption rate globally, with millions of individual investors participating in the market. Coinbase’s return to India expands its presence in Asia and paves the way for crypto legalization in an up-and-coming market. FIU approval, therefore, is not just an essential step for Coinbase extending its base in India, but also, an important step for cryptocurrency adoption worldwide.

data.title: City Developments In-House Legal Battle Concludes
data.description:
City Developments announced Wednesday the resolution of legal proceedings initiated by certain directors against others, bringing an end to a period of internal strife, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Following the conclusion of the proceedings, Kwek Leng Beng remains the company's executive chairman, while Sherman Kwek also remains the group CEO. There are also no changes within the board.

The company said it will now continue to focus on strengthening the business and aims to maximize shareholder value.

data.title: Can Fin Homes to issue over 2-year bonds, bankers say
data.description:
India's Can Fin Homes plans to raise 16 billion rupees ($183.80 million), including a with a greenshoe option of 12 billion rupees by selling bonds maturing in two year and two months, three bankers said on Thursday.

The company has invited coupon and commitment bids from bankers and investors for the issue on Monday, they said.

The company did not immediately reply to a Reuters email for comment.

China Medical System rises on drug clinical trials approval
data.description:
** Shares of China Medical System Holdings rise 4% to HK$8.54, their highest since February 27

** Stock set for the biggest one-day rise since February 26; on track for fourth straight session of gains

** The Chinese medicine producer says China's National Medical Products Administration has granted drug clinical trials approval to its Cardiac Myosin Inhibitor CMS-D003, which is a targeted therapy for hypertrophic cardiomyopathy

** Co, with market capitaliation of HK$20.64 bln ($2.66 bln), says CMS-D003 can also be developed for the treatment of heart failure with preserved ejection fraction in the future

** Hong Kong's healthcare index (.HSCIH) and Hang Seng Index both slip 0.7%

** Stock up 8.7% YTD

($1 = 7.7713 Hong Kong dollars)

data.title: Goodricke Group Completes Sale of Chulsa Tea Estate
data.description:
Goodricke Group has completed the sale of its Chulsa Tea Estate along with the assets in it, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

In mid-February, the company agreed to sell the Chulsa Tea Estate for 181.1 million Indian rupees.

data.title: Helios Energy Seeks Up to AU$3.6 Million via Discounted Placement, Entitlement Offer; Shares Fall 19%
data.description:
Helios Energy is raising up to AU$3.6 million via a placement and an entitlement offer, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company said it received firm commitments to raise AU$1 million via the placement of roughly 142.9 million shares at an issue price of AU$0.007 apiece. This represents a 56% discount to the company's last closing price on March 10, per the filing.

The remaining AU$2.6 million is being raised under the entitlement offer. The oil and gas explorer said Non-Executive Director Mark Lochtenberg committed to take up his entitlements in full as well as subscribe for up to AU$100,000 worth of shares under the shortfall. Managing Director Philipp Kin and Non-Executive Director John Cathcart also pledged to subscribe for up to AU$50,000 each under the shortfall.

Funds will be used for licensing and permitting renewals, technical geological, geotechnical, well design, and planning reclamation works related to the company's Presidio acreage, the filing stated.

Helios Energy's shares plunged nearly 19% in recent Thursday trade.

(04:08 GMT) Couche-Tard Doubles Down on Friendly Takeover of 7-Eleven Owner

By Megumi Fujikawa

TOKYO-Alimentation Couche-Tard will continue its friendly pursuit of Seven & i, executives said, sounding confident that they can overcome antitrust issues that could block the prospective acquisition in the U.S.

"We are continuing to pursue a friendly, mutually agreeable transaction" with the Japanese owner of the 7-Eleven convenience-store chain, Alain Bouchard, Couche-Tard's founder and executive chairman of the board, said at a news conference in Tokyo on Thursday.

"There is a clear path to regulatory approval," he said, while expressing frustration with the limited progress on broader deal discussions with Seven & i.

Couche-Tard Chief Executive Alex Miller said he has no plan for store closures or job cuts in Japan. "I think there's a lot of ways to increase profitability besides closing stores or firing people," he said, adding that listening to franchisees is one way to do so.

In a statement released earlier this week, the Canadian owner of Circle K reiterated its commitment to the deal, adding that there is considerable uncertainty around whether Seven & i's latest business plan can deliver value to shareholders. It also expressed disappointment that the engagement with Seven & i has been mostly focused on U.S. regulatory approval.

In September, Seven & i rejected an initial $39 billion buyout bid from Couche-Tard, saying the proposal underestimated the company's value and citing a possible antitrust challenge. Couche-Tard later raised its offer to about $47 billion.

The Canadian company said this week it resubmitted a revised, yen-denominated, nonbinding proposal on Jan. 24.

In a statement on a website, the company said it remains confident that its proposal is "the most attractive for Seven & i, its customers, franchisees, and the shareholders of both companies."

The 7-Eleven parent last week named its first American chief executive, former Walmart executive Stephen Hayes Dacus, and announced a plan to split its North American convenience stores into a separate listed company by the second half of next year.

data.title: XRP Bulls Ready to Charge—Upside Break May Spark Rally
data.description:
XRP price started a fresh recovery wave above the $2.00 zone. The price is now showing positive signs and might clear the $2.250 resistance zone.

  • XRP price started a fresh recovery wave above the $2.120 resistance zone.
  • The price is now trading above $2.150 and the 100-hourly Simple Moving Average.
  • There is a short-term bullish trend line forming with support at $2.188 on the hourly chart of the XRP/USD pair (data source from Kraken).
  • The pair might continue to move up if it clears the $2.250 resistance zone.

XRP Price Eyes Upside Break

XRP price remained supported and started a recovery wave from the $1.90 zone, like Bitcoin and Ethereum. The price was able to clear the $2.00 and $2.050 resistance levels.

There was a move above the $2.120 resistance. The price surpassed the 50% Fib retracement level of the downward wave from the $2.365 swing high to the $1.90 low. However, the bears are now active near the $2.250 resistance zone.

The price is now trading above $2.150 and the 100-hourly Simple Moving Average. There is also a short-term bullish trend line forming with support at $2.188 on the hourly chart of the XRP/USD pair.

On the upside, the price might face resistance near the $2.250 level. It is near the 76.4% Fib retracement level of the downward wave from the $2.365 swing high to the $1.90 low. The first major resistance is near the $2.3650 level.

The next resistance is $2.450. A clear move above the $2.450 resistance might send the price toward the $2.50 resistance. Any more gains might send the price toward the $2.550 resistance or even $2.650 in the near term. The next major hurdle for the bulls might be $2.80.

Another Decline?

If XRP fails to clear the $2.250 resistance zone, it could start another decline. Initial support on the downside is near the $2.1880 level and the trend line. The next major support is near the $2.120 level.

If there is a downside break and a close below the $2.120 level, the price might continue to decline toward the $2.050 support. The next major support sits near the $2.00 zone.

Technical Indicators

Hourly MACD – The MACD for XRP/USD is now gaining pace in the bullish zone.

Hourly RSI (Relative Strength Index) – The RSI for XRP/USD is now above the 50 level.

Major Support Levels – $2.180 and $2.120.

Major Resistance Levels – $2.250 and $2.350.
